name: D+Q 20-week adverse-event discontinuation result
arm_details: ClinicalTrials.gov participant flow reports 3/30 D+Q and 2/30 untreated-control participants did not complete the study. Drop/withdraw reasons include adverse event in 2 D+Q and 0 control participants, withdrawal by subject in 1 D+Q and 1 control participant, and protocol violation in 0 D+Q and 1 control participant. The publication safety narrative describes the D+Q discontinuations as QTc interval prolongation after dasatinib, fatigue and nonspecific side effects, and COVID concern.

group_values.group_label: D+Q not completed because of adverse event
Untreated control not completed because of adverse event

effect.raw_text: ClinicalTrials.gov participantFlowModule reports dropWithdraws: Adverse Event 2/30 D+Q and 0/30 control; Withdrawal by Subject 1/30 D+Q and 1/30 control; Protocol Violation 0/30 D+Q and 1/30 control. Publication safety section describes D+Q withdrawal reasons.

analysis.effect_note: No comparative discontinuation effect was calculated because the control adverse-event-discontinuation count is zero, discontinuation reasons are heterogeneous, and the publication narrative distinguishes QTc prolongation, fatigue/nonspecific side effects, COVID concern, and control early terminations.

event_specific_counts.interpretation: The registry participant-flow module reports two D+Q participants did not complete because of adverse event.
The registry participant-flow module reports zero untreated-control participants did not complete because of adverse event.

zero_handling.rationale: The registry provides an explicit zero for control adverse-event discontinuation, but this sparse discontinuation outcome is kept descriptive because no comparative effect or uncertainty was prespecified for the heterogeneous withdrawal reasons.

provenance.note: ClinicalTrials.gov participant flow reports not-completed counts and drop/withdraw reasons by arm.
Publication safety paragraph describes D+Q withdrawal reasons and control early terminations.

statement: Registry participant-flow extraction shows adverse-event discontinuation in 2 of 30 D+Q participants and 0 of 30 untreated-control participants, with total noncompletion of 3/30 and 2/30 respectively; publication text provides the D+Q withdrawal reasons.
caveats: The adverse-event discontinuation result should not be combined with preferred-term adverse-event rows without resolving overlap and endpoint definitions.
The publication narrative identifies individual reasons but does not provide a formal comparative discontinuation analysis.
The registry includes a fisetin arm outside this D+Q versus untreated-control extraction boundary.
